_about: Single source of truth for the jubuddy-HK isometric grid — read by BOTH the asset bake (game-tiles) and the future jubuddy-HK renderer (Part 0 engine TBD). Reconciles the 'Claude Code x 2.5D Isometric' prompt pack with this repo's actual Phase-2 assets (game-tiles/README.md). Pack example numbers (128x64, crisp pixel) are superseded by reality below.

spriteCanvas.note: actual slice size in game-tiles/iso/tiles.json; the 2:1 diamond is 256x128, the extra 32px is top overhang for raised content + spawn/goal markers

rendering.note: LOCKED: soft pictorial (yok_iso_hk_soft_pictorial), NOT crisp pixel-art. So the pack's pixelArt{quantizePalette,nearestNeighbor,integerScaleOnly} block does NOT apply.

_doc.note: JSON has no comments; these are the authoritative formulas every renderer MUST use (corrected from the pack's Part 3 — the earlier 'Z = Y x tileWidth' was wrong).
_doc.gridToScreen: screenX = originX + (gx - gy) * (tileWidth / 2); screenY = originY + (gx + gy) * (tileHeight / 2) - z * elevationStep
_doc.drawOrder2D: depthKey = gx + gy (larger = nearer the viewer = drawn later); tiebreak by z, then a stable id. A multi-cell tall prop sorts by max(gx+gy) of its footprint, or split into per-cell sub-sprites.
_doc.engine3D: In a 3D / z-buffer engine (Three.js-orthographic, Unity, Godot) do NOT compute depthKey — place each sprite at the world position from (gx, gy, z) and let the depth buffer / Sorting Group / Y-sort resolve occlusion. Prefer this when tall HK towers next to low buildings break painter's-algorithm sort.

consumers.bake: jubit-hk-tiles game-tiles/ (ground = scripts/make_ground_tiles.py deterministic; props = central_render_bake.py --transparent → hand-clean)
consumers.renderer: jubuddy-HK (spin-off of jubuddy-game) — engine per Part 0 (Web/TS Pixi|Three-iso · Unity · Godot, undecided)

props: game-tiles/props/ (per-prop transparent sprites; a props-manifest.json with per-prop {anchorX,anchorY,footprint,heightInLevels} is the documented next gap for the renderer)
references: Claude Code x 2.5D Isometric — Tailored Prompt & Command Pack (Part 1B / Part 3)
